_meta.description: Page manifest for the focal-4 HI viewer. Each entry corresponds to one HI page-side (left or right half of a 2-page PDF spread). The 'sequence' field is the navigation order in the viewer (left-to-right within a domain, then next domain). The 'book_page' is the printed page number visible at the bottom margin of the page.

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第二 (鹿兒島)
pages.page_summary_en: The Satsuma (Kagoshima) entry's Table 2 page shows the kokudaka aggregates: sōdaka of 869,594 koku (with a 琉球國高引除 annotation indicating Ryūkyū tribute is excluded), the breakdown into 損高 (loss-high) and the remaining 高, plus current rice yield (現石) and zatsuzei (miscellaneous tax) figures including the Ryūkyū sugar monopoly revenue.

pages.page_topic: demographics — population, households, shizoku / ashigaru-and-below counts
pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第二 (高知) — 鹿兒島藩データ
pages.page_header_en: Domain Survey Table 2 — Satsuma demographics (printed on the 高知/Kōchi page)
pages.page_summary_en: Satsuma's demographic block — total population 896,808 and 174,528 households, of which 43,119 shizoku households / 192,949 shizoku persons (~22% of the domain, an exceptionally high samurai share from Satsuma's gōshi system) and 107,738 / 579,405 in the 足輕以下 ('foot-soldier and below') class. A Ryūkyū-administration sub-block and the Ryūkyū tribute-rice figure (8,669 koku) also print here. The whole block overflowed to the RIGHT of the ○高知藩 (Tosa) marker, onto Tosa's Table-2 page — Tosa's own demographics are on the next page (p90R).

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第八 (鹿児島)
pages.page_topic: officers + military, with explicit non-disclosure annotation
pages.page_summary_en: Satsuma's HI Table 8 entry is essentially a non-disclosure: just the ○鹿兒島藩 marker followed by 書ナラス ('not specified / not written'). This is a TEACHING page for right-to-left page-spill. The military numbers to the RIGHT of the ○鹿兒島藩 marker — 兵卒人員 1,568; 士等役員 1,106; 隊士 464; the four councillor counts (1+9+21+16 = 47) — sit to the RIGHT of the ○鹿兒島藩 marker, so in R→L reading order they are read BEFORE it and belong to the PRECEDING domain, 金澤藩 (Kanazawa), whose entry continues onto this page from vol 2 PDF p162. The data table shows only Satsuma's actual disclosure: 書ナラス. To the LEFT of Satsuma's marker the ○高知藩 (Tosa) entry begins, with a full named officer roster — the contrast underlines just how total Satsuma's refusal was. Combined with its real ~16,000-Spencer-rifle force, Satsuma was the most opaque military presence in the 1870 survey.

Chōshū's kokudaka page: 草高 369,411 + 外高 (new-field uplift) 618,593 = 合高 988,004 (exact; the 合高 carries an editorial ◎ note marking it as outside the original book). Also the main tax — 正租 現米 206,910 koku and 正租 錢 116,190 kan-mon — plus 雜稅 in rice (14,553) and silver (890.86) and an external wax-tax of 480 ryō. Status-class counts to the RIGHT of the ○山口藩 marker belong to the preceding domain; Chōshū's demographics are on the facing page.

Chōshū's status register (not the full commoner population), laid out in two horizontal bands. Top: total 戸數 17,714 and total 人口 66,463, with status sub-counts — 内 士族 3,000 households / 11,589 persons, 内 卒族 3,991 / 10,362, 内 陪臣 6,157, plus 社家, 末庵, 盲僧, 非人. Bottom: 寺院 1,165 (incl. 5 穢多寺), 山伏 92, 穢多 2,675, 茶洗 6, and the male/female split for each population figure (人口 男 36,240 / 女 30,223; 士族 男 5,829 / 女 5,760; 卒族 男 5,567 / 女 4,795 — each sum-validated).

Chōshū's Table-9 military disclosure — a stark contrast with Satsuma's 書ナラス. The land force: 17 infantry battalions of 433 men each (= 7,361 infantry), 6½ artillery batteries (8 guns / 93 men each), and one 93-man cavalry company. The navy: four wooden steam warships, each listed with its original foreign name — 第一丁卯丸 (ex-Penta), 第二丁卯丸 (ex-Asonda), 鳳翔丸, and 乙丑丸 (ex-Conchin). The ○山口藩 marker and the senior officials (知事・大参事…) are on the facing right half (p204-R); the tail of the 権少参事 roster appears here.

Tosa's Table-2 page: 草高 495,486 koku, given as a five-year average (甲子ヨリ戊辰 = 1864–1868, through the Boshin War). Current rice 現米 189,431 + 此米 3,587 = 總計 193,018 (exact). The tax in gold 税金 28,697 ryō and in silver 税銀 28,697 kanme share the same mantissa as currency equivalents. Total 戸數 93,736. A long 諸産物 list catalogues Tosa's named products (no quantities). The marker is on the facing page; the population total (495,936) is on the demographics page.

pages.page_topic: population + status classes (with M/F) + standing army
pages.page_summary_en: Tosa's demographic page. Total 人口 495,936 (no M/F printed for the grand total). Status classes, each with male/female sub-totals: 士族 7,269 households / 30,506 persons (男 15,138 / 女 15,368); 卒族 3,073 / 17,681 (男 10,954 / 女 6,727); 穢多 3,396 / 16,894 (男 8,609 / 女 8,285); the Shinto-priest/ascetic class 1,016 / 4,214; and 476 temples with 1,572 clergy — every M/F pair sum-validated. The page closes with Tosa's army: 常備兵 6,600 + 非常兵 2,400 = 9,000 (合九千人).

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第八 (鹿兒島・高知)
pages.page_summary_en: Tosa's Table-8 officer roster — printed to the LEFT of the ○高知藩 marker on the SAME scan where Satsuma's entry is the single non-statement 書ナラス. Where Satsuma disclosed nothing, Tosa names its hierarchy: 知事 (governor) Yamauchi, 大参事 Fukao Hiroto, 権大参事 Gotō Jozan, 少参事 Saitō Yakuma, and a long 権少参事 roster beginning with Konami Gorō and including future national figures (Kataoka Kenkichi, Tani Tateki). The roster and the 海陸兵員 9,000 total continue on p163-L and p164 — the same force shown on the demographics page as 常備 6,600 + 非常 2,400.

pages.page_topic: kokudaka via 'two-mouth combined / 5-yr average' + main tax
pages.page_summary_en: Saga (entered only in vol2) uses an unusual phrasing for its combined yield: 貮口合 平均 = 724,739.3152 koku, the five-year average of 高 211,519 + 新田高 513,580 — Saga led the Edo period in new-field reclamation, so its real yield far exceeds the ~357k formal rating. Main tax: 正租米 213,478 and 取納現米 217,789 koku, plus a silver tax of 444.1 kanme (with its rice-equivalent 但米 ≈ 4,441 koku) and a tiny 10-ryō miscellaneous gold tax. The 永 figure right of the ○佐嘉藩 marker belongs to the preceding domain.

Saga's demographic totals and products. Total 戸數 68,710 and total 人員 354,450 — male 183,517 + female 170,933 = 354,450, sum-validated — plus 士族戸數 5,273. The product block lists Saga's output with quantities, including the famous Hizen coal (石炭 36,000,000 jin), Arita/Imari ceramics (陶器 20,000+ bales), rice 92,719 koku, tea, wax, sōmen, sake, and dried shrimp, and a cash figure of 99,831 kan-mon. The shizoku/sotsu PERSON counts are on the facing left half (saga_status).

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第六 (佐嘉 → 佐倉)
pages.page_header_en: Domain Survey Table 6 — Saga status classes, then the Sakura boundary
pages.page_summary_en: The left half of Saga's demographic spread: the status-class PERSON counts that complete the totals on the facing page. 士族 23,823 persons; 卒 9,369 households / 43,488 persons; 僧 1,032 / 4,412; shrine personnel 195 / 850; and a 'shrine count not specified' (社數不詳) note — each person figure carries an internal male/female split. The ○佐倉藩 marker then begins the NEXT domain (Sakura-han, a Hotta domain in Shimōsa), whose own kokudaka prints to its left — the same right-to-left domain-boundary signal seen across the survey.

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第九 (佐賀 → 佐倉)
pages.page_header_en: Domain Survey Table 9 — Saga's army (on the Sakura page)
pages.page_summary_en: Saga's army — found at native resolution in 2026, overturning the long belief that Hansei Ichiran recorded no force for Saga (the search had used the old-kanji 佐嘉, while Table 9 prints 佐賀). Its ○佐賀藩 marker and officer roster are on the facing page (p.429); the army spills onto the top of this page and reads right-to-left, ending just before the ○佐倉藩 (Sakura) marker. Six battalions, 2,972 personnel (officers 864 + artillery 180 + infantry 1,440 + artillery-privates 108 + infantry-privates 260 + bandsmen 120) and 36 cannon. A 少参事以下書ナラス note shows the junior-official roster was withheld.

pages.page_topic: kokudaka (formal vs real) + sundry taxes + male population
pages.page_summary_en: Kumamoto (Higo), the Hosokawa house. The marker carries the formal rating 高 540,000 koku, but the real assessed 草高 is 786,016 — a 246,016-koku new-field uplift (但内). Main tax 正税 = 304,838 koku; sundry taxes (諸税) in rice (73,363), salt (1,965), gold (2,615 ryō), and 503,469 jin of wax-tree berries (the raw material for Higo's renowned vegetable-wax industry). The male population 361,024 is recorded here; the female count and grand total are on the demographics pages.

The male population broken out by status class: farmers 278,846 (Kumamoto, unlike some domains, registers commoners), sotsu 37,219, merchants 18,738, shizoku 8,050, former rear-vassals 8,259, clergy 3,167, eta 3,495, plus smaller groups — summing exactly to the 361,024 male total. The total female population (355,783) and the female shizoku (8,000) also print here; the female-by-class breakdown and the grand totals (人口合 719,990 persons, 戸數 148,852 households) are on the facing left half (p184L), and households-by-class + shrines + a six-ship list are on p185R. Shizoku male + female ≈ 16,050 — behind Kumamoto's high retainer density (≈111.8 per 1,000 koku).

Kumamoto's disclosed force: a samurai corps of 2 battalions (576), a rifle corps of 4 battalions (1,152), and 6 artillery units (288 gunners, 24 French 4.5-pounder guns) — about 2,016 troops in all. Its navy is striking: six vessels, most foreign-built steamers — 萬里丸 (ex-Cosmos White, French), 凌雲丸 (ex-Granada, British iron), 奮迅丸 (ex-Felicia, iron), a sailing ship, 神風丸 (ex-Kagoshima, British), and 泰運丸 (ex-Corea, American). The ○熊本藩 marker and the named officials (知事 + 参事) are on the facing right half (p200R); the ○久留米藩 (Kurume) marker at the left begins the next domain.

Kanazawa (金澤藩, the Maeda house) — the largest non-Tokugawa domain in Japan, the legendary 'million-koku Kaga'. Real 草高 1,353,353 koku, of which the productive 内高 is 1,025,020; the gap (込高新田改出 328,333) is reclaimed new field. Main tax 正租米 633,134 koku, with large coin taxes (錢 258,590 kan, 雜稅錢 75,634 kan). The headline demographics also print here: 戸數 198,801 households and 人口 996,069 — the largest household and population counts in the survey — of which 士族 7,797 households / 28,683 persons. Against ~1M people the shizoku share is modest (~2.9%; ≈55.9 retainers per 1,000 koku), far below Satsuma or Kumamoto. The retainer sub-classes and religious establishment continue on the next page (p88R).

pages.page_topic: retainer/dependent sub-classes (with M/F) + religious establishment + military summary
pages.page_summary_en: This page continues Kanazawa's entry from the kokudaka page (p87L), breaking the retainer-and-dependent population into its sub-classes — 卒族 (lower-samurai) 27,038 persons / 9,703 households, 中間小者 (attendants and menials) 5,938 / 2,699, and 藤内等 (the outcaste classes, 但 穢多之類) 11,695 / 2,243 — then the religious establishment (神社 407 with 社人 1,799; 寺 2,047 with 僧 13,326). Every population column carries an internal male/female split, each sum-validated against its total (e.g. 卒族 男 14,655 + 女 12,383 = 27,038). The left margin adds a military-reform summary — 步兵 5 battalions, 砲兵 3 units, officer cadre 1,106 and rank-and-file 兵卒人員 1,568, plus 2 steamships and 3 sailing vessels — the same force detailed on the military page.

Kanazawa's Table-8 entry is spread across three half-pages and is a clean teaching case for right-to-left page-spill. The ○金澤藩 marker and the senior officials (知事・大参事・権大参事) are on the facing right half (p162R). THIS half (p162L) carries the junior-official roster (少参事 / 権少参事 — Honda Shigurehei and eight others) and the disclosed corps: 步兵 5 battalions (320 riflemen each, 516 officers/staff) and 砲兵 3 units (144 men each, 186 officers/staff). The personnel TOTALS — 兵卒人員 1,568, 士等役員 1,106, 隊士 464, and the four councillor counts (1+9+21+16 = 47) — overflow to the RIGHT of the next domain's marker on p163R, the very same scan shown on the Satsuma military page. The printed page number 三百一 (301) sits in the bottom margin.

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第四 (名古屋)
pages.page_topic: kokudaka — reformed survey, sub-fief highs, and the 8-bu tax note
pages.page_summary_en: Nagoya (名古屋藩), the senior Tokugawa shinpan (the Owari-Tokugawa house, with scattered holdings across Owari, Mino, Ōmi and Settsu). Its kokudaka page reflects the June-1870 (庚午六月) reformed survey: current 高 853,125 koku — among the largest in the country — from which sundry deductions (諸引高 40,735) leave a residual 殘高 of 778,896; the main tax delivered (租納高) is 261,250, plus an 'external figure' (外數) of 282,150 koku of additional surveyed land. The page also itemises Owari's semi-autonomous sub-fiefs as separate 所務高 entries (e.g. Ishikawa Tahachirō's 19,170 and the 10,000 koku returned by the Imao-han governor), and carries the 此八分出 ('8-bu output') note explaining how the delivered tax-rice was recalculated. The printed page number 二百八十七 (287) is in the bottom margin.
